name: Flash-Cooked Lamb with Leeks
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Boneless leg of lamb, shank portion
8 Garlic cloves, peeled and very thinly sliced
4 cup Leeks, white part only, cut into fine julienne shreds
recipeInstructions:
From Nina Simonds' "China Express" (William Morrow, $25).
Using a sharp knife or cleaver, trim the lamb of fat and gristle and remove any tendons. Separate the muscles of the meat and remove any filmy skin. Cut the meat across the grain into thin slices about 1/6 inch wide and 1 1/2 inches long.
Place the slices in a bowl. Add soy sauce, wine, sugar, ginger root, and cornstarch and toss lightly to coat. Cover with plastic wrap and marinate for 30 minutes in the refrigerator. Prepare the sauce and set aside.
Heat a wok or skillet. Add 1/4 cup corn oil and heat to about 400°F. Add the lamb slices and stir-fry over high heat, stirring continuously, until the meat loses its red color and separates. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain in a colander. Clean out the skillet.
Reheat the skillet, add 1 tablespoon corn oil and 1 tablespoon sesame oil. Heat until very hot. Add garlic and leeks and stir-fry over high heat about 1 1/2 minutes, until the leeks are just tender. Add the cooked lamb and stir-fry sauce and toss lightly to coat. Transfer to a serving platter.
